name: Puerco Adobado
recipeInstructions:
1. Place pork on a rack in a broiling pan, about 6 inches from the heat source. Broil until rare, about 5 to 6 minutes.
2. Transfer pork to a 3-quart saucepan.
3. Add water, vinegar, soy sauce, garlic, and pepper; stir well.
4. Bring mixture to a boil over high heat.
5. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until pork is fork-tender, about 30 to 40 minutes.
6. Using a slotted spoon, transfer pork to a plate and set aside.
7. Increase heat to high and cook pan juices in a separate saucepan, stirring occasionally, until reduced by half.
8. Remove from heat and reserve.
9. In a 10-inch nonstick skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat.
10. Add pork cubes and cook, turning frequently, until well-browned on all sides, about 4 to 5 minutes.
11. Stir in reserved pan juices; cook until sauce is heated through, about 1 minute.
